$comment: macOS-specific overlay (Tauri v2 auto-merges this with tauri.conf.json on Darwin builds).
On Linux + Windows the engine ships as a flat sidecar binary (matrx-engine-<triple>)
declared in the main tauri.conf.json under bundle.externalBin. Tauri places it in
On macOS we ship the engine as a Helper .app bundle (Matrx Engine.app) so that
Activity Monitor displays it as 'Matrx Engine' with its own icon — instead of
inheriting the parent app's name and icon (which is what happened when the engine
binary lived directly inside the parent's Contents/MacOS/).
1. Removes 'sidecar/matrx-engine' from externalBin (replacing the array — Tauri
merges sibling overlays by replacing arrays, not appending).
2. Adds bundle.macOS.files to copy desktop/src-tauri/sidecar/Matrx Engine.app
into the parent's Contents/Frameworks/Matrx Engine.app/. Tauri's bundler
auto-codesigns nested code in /Frameworks (PR #8259), so the helper is
signed with the parent's identity and included in notarization automatically.
